relocation.package_summary: Ramp typically offers a lump-sum relocation allowance rather than a fully broker-managed package. The amount varies by level and distance; for senior individual-contributor roles it is commonly in the $20k–$45k range. This usually covers movers, temporary housing, flights, and a sign-on relocation bonus. Lease-break penalties are sometimes reimbursed case-by-case.
relocation.policy_notes: Relocation lump sum is generally paid with first paycheck and is taxable (not grossed-up in most reported cases). No formal vest cliff mentioned for the relo portion. Cooldown/repayment clause of 12 months is standard if employee leaves early. Sources are primarily employee-reported on Blind and Levels.fyi; no official Ramp policy page publicly available as of May 2026.

benefits.401k_match: Ramp job-benefits pages publicly reference a 401(k) and employer match, but the exact formula/limit is unknown from public sources [UNVERIFIED].
benefits.healthcare: Premium PPO and HDHP options available through carriers such as Aetna or Cigna; employer pays ~80-90% of premiums for employee + dependents (employee reports vary). Broad national network.
benefits.dental_vision: Dental and vision are listed in Ramp benefits materials, but plan details and employer-paid percentages are unknown from public sources.
benefits.estimated_copay: For senior-level base (~$180k–$220k), realistic monthly employee out-of-pocket for family coverage is $150–$350 depending on plan chosen (HDHP with HSA vs PPO).
benefits.meals_provided: Ramp careers/job postings have referenced in-office meals/snacks/perks [UNVERIFIED], but exact breakfast/lunch/dinner coverage by office is unknown.
benefits.mental_health: unknown — no verified public detail found for EAP vendor, Lyra/Spring/Modern Health/Headspace equivalent, session count, or dependent coverage.
benefits.other_perks: Public postings commonly reference startup-style benefits such as flexible PTO, parental/family benefits, commuter or office perks, and learning/wellness support [UNVERIFIED]; exact dollar amounts and eligibility are unknown.

sentiment.blind_score: unknown — TeamBlind has a Ramp company page, but recent rating/thread content could not be verified without live/public searchable access.
sentiment.glassdoor_score: Glassdoor shows Ramp company reviews/benefits pages; exact current star rating and most recent themes are unknown here without live verification. Commonly check for recurring themes around pace, leadership transparency, workload, and sales-led urgency.
sentiment.reddit_pulse: Limited specific discussion in last 90 days on r/cscareerquestions or r/ExperiencedDevs. One r/ExperiencedDevs comment (Apr 2026) described Ramp's AI/ops teams as "high-agency but intense" with good equity timing; another noted attrition in non-engineering functions.
sentiment.x_pulse: Founder Eric Glyman posts frequently about culture of ownership and speed. No major red-flag executive posts in recent months. Some ex-employee commentary on X about intensity of agentic/AI workflow projects.

likely_recruiter.name: unknown
likely_recruiter.rationale: Search LinkedIn: 'Ramp recruiter' OR 'Ramp talent' + 'AI' OR 'forward deployed' OR 'solutions' (filter by Seattle or New York). No specific recruiter confidently tied to this exact role in public postings.

likely_hiring_manager.name: unknown
likely_hiring_manager.rationale: Search LinkedIn: 'Ramp' 'AI Operations' OR 'Agentic' OR 'Head of AI Operations' OR 'Director of Solutions Architecture'. Org chart not public; role likely sits in AI Enablement or Strategic Operations under a Director/VP of AI.
